question.text: STT on the sale of option contracts (where option is sold/written) is:

answer.text: 0.10% of the option premium, payable by the seller
0.02% of the contract value, payable by the seller
0.125% of the option premium, payable by the buyer
0.10% of the settlement price, payable by both buyer and seller

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** STT on sale of options: - Rate: 0.10% (10 paise per Rs. 100) - Payable on: the option premium amount (not the contract value or settlement price) - Payable by: the seller of the option
This is different from STT on exercise of options (0.125% on settlement price, payable by purchaser).
**The Trap:** Note the different bases: futures STT (0.02%) is on traded price, option sale STT (0.10%) is on premium, and option exercise STT (0.125%) is on settlement price. Different bases with different rates.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Distinguishing STT rates and bases across different derivative transactions is a key exam skill in Series VIII.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Option sale STT: 0.10% on premium (seller pays). Option exercise: 0.125% on settlement price (buyer pays). Futures sale: 0.02% on traded price (seller pays).
